specification.topic-statement: The HTML parsing wrapper exposes the HTML document parsing and document freeing operations through the link-seam mechanism so tests can substitute or intercept them.

scope.in-scope: The two wrapped HTML operations: parsing an in-memory HTML byte buffer into a document tree, and releasing a parsed document's memory. The build-mode selection between inline and weak-symbol form.
scope.boundary:
The MOCKABLE attribute and the inline-vs-weak-symbol compilation strategy are shared infrastructure.
In release builds the wrappers are static inline; in debug builds they are weak symbols that tests can override.
The actual document parsing and tree construction are performed by an external HTML parsing library. This wrapper does not modify those behaviors.

The HTML parsing wrapper header explicitly includes the HTTP client wrapper header as a dependency, making the full HTTP wrapper surface available to any translation unit that includes this header. The HTTP wrappers themselves are documented separately.

behavior.parse-html-buffer: Parses an HTML document from a caller-supplied byte buffer. The caller provides the buffer, its size in bytes, a URL string used as the document's base URI, a character encoding name, and an integer options bitmask. Returns an opaque document handle on success, or null on failure. All arguments are forwarded to the underlying library unchanged; no preprocessing or validation occurs.
behavior.free-document: Releases all memory associated with a parsed HTML document. Accepts the opaque document handle returned by the parse operation. Forwards the handle to the underlying library's free routine unchanged. Returns nothing. No null-check is performed before forwarding; passing null is undefined behavior inherited from the underlying library.

notable.reason: not included in the aggregation umbrella

umbrella-exclusion.notable: This wrapper family is not pulled in by the master wrapper umbrella header. Translation units that require HTML parsing operations must include this header directly. Every other wrapper family is collected by the umbrella; this one is the sole exception.
